verses.text: Paul, an apostle 'separated unto the gospel of God,' which concerns His Son, 'declared to be the Son of God... by the resurrection.' The theme set. The whole letter is about the gospel of Christ.
Paul, an apostle 'separated unto the gospel of God,' which concerns His Son, 'declared to be the Son of God... by the resurrection.' The theme set. The whole letter is about the gospel of Christ.
Paul, an apostle 'separated unto the gospel of God,' which concerns His Son, 'declared to be the Son of God... by the resurrection.' The theme set. The whole letter is about the gospel of Christ.
Paul, an apostle 'separated unto the gospel of God,' which concerns His Son, 'declared to be the Son of God... by the resurrection.' The theme set. The whole letter is about the gospel of Christ.
Grace and apostleship are given 'for obedience to the faith among all nations'; he greets the called at Rome. Wide call. The gospel goes to every nation.
Grace and apostleship are given 'for obedience to the faith among all nations'; he greets the called at Rome. Wide call. The gospel goes to every nation.
Grace and apostleship are given 'for obedience to the faith among all nations'; he greets the called at Rome. Wide call. The gospel goes to every nation.
He thanks God for their faith, 'spoken of throughout the whole world,' and prays for them always. Grateful prayer. He rejoices in the faith of others.
He thanks God for their faith, 'spoken of throughout the whole world,' and prays for them always. Grateful prayer. He rejoices in the faith of others.
He thanks God for their faith, 'spoken of throughout the whole world,' and prays for them always. Grateful prayer. He rejoices in the faith of others.
He longs to see them, to impart a gift, that they be 'comforted together by the mutual faith.' Shared blessing. Believers strengthen one another.
He longs to see them, to impart a gift, that they be 'comforted together by the mutual faith.' Shared blessing. Believers strengthen one another.
He longs to see them, to impart a gift, that they be 'comforted together by the mutual faith.' Shared blessing. Believers strengthen one another.
I am 'debtor' to all, and ready to preach the gospel at Rome also. A holy debt. The gospel owed to him he owes to others.
I am 'debtor' to all, and ready to preach the gospel at Rome also. A holy debt. The gospel owed to him he owes to others.
'I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation'; 'the just shall live by faith.' The great text. Faith in the gospel saves and sustains.
'I am not ashamed of the gospel of Christ: for it is the power of God unto salvation'; 'the just shall live by faith.' The great text. Faith in the gospel saves and sustains.
God's wrath is revealed against ungodliness; His power is plain in creation, leaving men 'without excuse.' No excuse. The world's Maker is seen in His works.
God's wrath is revealed against ungodliness; His power is plain in creation, leaving men 'without excuse.' No excuse. The world's Maker is seen in His works.
God's wrath is revealed against ungodliness; His power is plain in creation, leaving men 'without excuse.' No excuse. The world's Maker is seen in His works.
Knowing God, they glorified Him not, but changed His glory into images. Foolish exchange. They traded the living God for lifeless idols.
Knowing God, they glorified Him not, but changed His glory into images. Foolish exchange. They traded the living God for lifeless idols.
Knowing God, they glorified Him not, but changed His glory into images. Foolish exchange. They traded the living God for lifeless idols.
God 'gave them up' to uncleanness, who 'worshipped... the creature more than the Creator.' Given over. Sin, persisted in, is its own judgment.
God 'gave them up' to uncleanness, who 'worshipped... the creature more than the Creator.' Given over. Sin, persisted in, is its own judgment.
He gave them up to vile affections, against nature, receiving 'that recompence... which was meet.' Reaping shame. Sin against nature brings its own ruin.
He gave them up to vile affections, against nature, receiving 'that recompence... which was meet.' Reaping shame. Sin against nature brings its own ruin.
He gave them over to 'a reprobate mind,' filled with unrighteousness, envy, and pride. A darkened mind. Refusing God darkens the whole soul.
He gave them over to 'a reprobate mind,' filled with unrighteousness, envy, and pride. A darkened mind. Refusing God darkens the whole soul.
He gave them over to 'a reprobate mind,' filled with unrighteousness, envy, and pride. A darkened mind. Refusing God darkens the whole soul.
He gave them over to 'a reprobate mind,' filled with unrighteousness, envy, and pride. A darkened mind. Refusing God darkens the whole soul.
Knowing such deserve death, they not only do them but 'have pleasure in them that do them.' Hardened. They delight in the sin they should mourn.
